retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Specificity
retrieved_docs.content: Specificity is the percentage of true negatives out of all subjects who do not have a disease or condition [4] . In other words, it is the ability of the test or instrument to obtain normal range or negative results for a person who does not have a disease. [2] The formula to determine specificity is the following: Specificity=(True Negatives (D))/(True Negatives (D)+False Positives (B))

retrieved_docs.title: Drug Testing -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: As with all testing, false negatives and false positives are a possibility. False negatives are defined as a negative test result despite the presence of a given xenobiotic. A false positive is defined as a positive test result despite no xenobiotic present in a sample. Common false positives are described in the testing of amphetamines and include medications such as selegiline, bupropion, and pseudoephedrine. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Specificity
retrieved_docs.content: Sensitivity and specificity are inversely related: as sensitivity increases, specificity tends to decrease , and vice versa. [3] [6] Highly sensitive tests will lead to positive findings for patients with a disease, whereas highly specific tests will show patients without a finding having no disease. [6] Sensitivity and specificity should always merit consideration together to provide a holistic picture of a diagnostic test. [7] Next, it is important to understand PPVs and NPVs.

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Sensitivity
retrieved_docs.content: Sensitivity is the proportion of true positives tests out of all patients with a condition. [4] In other words, it is the ability of a test or instrument to yield a positive result for a subject that has that disease. [2] The ability to correctly classify a test is essential, and the equation for sensitivity is the following: Sensitivity=(True Positives (A))/(True Positives (A)+False Negatives (C)) Sensitivity does not allow providers to understand individuals who tested positive but did not have the disease. [5] False positives are a consideration through measurements of specificity and PPV.

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- PPV and NPV
retrieved_docs.content: PPVs determine, out of all of the positive findings, how many are true positives; NPVs determine, out of all of the negative findings, how many are true negatives. As the value increases toward 100, it approaches a ‘gold standard.’ [3] The formulas for PPV and NPV are below. Positive Predictive Value=(True Positives (A))/(True Positives (A)+False Positives (B)) Negative Predictive Value=(True Negatives (D))/(True Negatives (D)+False Negatives(C))

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Likelihood Ratios
retrieved_docs.content: Likelihood ratios (LRs) represent another statistical tool to understand diagnostic tests. LRs allow providers to determine how much the utilization of a particular test will alter the probability. [4] A positive likelihood ratio, or LR+, is the “probability that a positive test would be expected in a patient divided by the probability that a positive test would be expected in a patient without a disease.”. [4] In other words, an LR+ is the true positivity rate divided by the false positivity rate [3] .  A negative likelihood ratio or LR-, is “the probability of a patient testing negative who has a disease divided by the probability of a patient testing negative who does not have a disease.”. [4] Unlike predictive values, and similar to sensitivity and specificity, likelihood ratios are not impacted by disease prevalence. [9] The formulas for the likelihood ratios are below. Positive Likelihood Ratio=Sensitivity/(1-Specificity) Negative Likelihood Ratio=(1- Sensitivity)/Specificity

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Drug Testing -- Testing Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: Knowledge of laboratory methods involved with urine drug testing help to facilitate test result interpretation. Drug testing is often a two-step process: screening and confirmation. Screening tests via biological samples are usually first administered for speculative testing. In some settings, a confirmative test is unnecessary if the patient confirms test findings. However, a definitive or confirmatory test is required if the patient contradicts speculative test results.

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Benefits of Diagnostic Testing
retrieved_docs.content: The utilization of diagnostic tests in patient care settings must be guided by evidence. Unfortunately, many order tests without considering the evidence to support them. [1] Sensitivity and specificity are essential indicators of test accuracy and allow healthcare providers to determine the appropriateness of the diagnostic tool. [2] Providers should utilize diagnostic tests with the proper level of confidence in the results derived from known sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive values (PPV), negative predictive values (NPV), positive likelihood ratios, and negative likelihood ratios. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Fecal Occult Blood Test -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Using a povidone-iodine antiseptic solution is associated with false-positive results with guaiac-based assays. [55] In-vitro studies showed that as little as 0.005 mL of 1:1000 dilution of this solution would give a false-positive result. This interference is due to the iodine in the antiseptic causing oxidation of the alpha-guaiaconic acid. [56]

retrieved_docs.title: Tuberculosis Screening -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Interreader variability should be highlighted as a significant cause of false-positive and false-negative results. Results have been shown to vary up to 15% between different personnel interpreting the test. [8] This figure increases when those with little prior experience read the test. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Drug Testing -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: It is essential that clinicians understand the testing methodology of various drug testing modalities as well as their associated sensitivity, specificity, and significance of false-negative and false-positive results.

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Application Exercise
retrieved_docs.content: Example: A healthcare provider utilizes a blood test to determine whether or not patients will have a disease. The results are the following: A total of 1,000 individuals had their blood tested. Four hundred twenty-seven individuals had positive findings, and 573 individuals had negative findings. Out of the 427 individuals who had positive findings, 369 of them had the disease. Out of the 573 individuals who had negative findings, 558 did not have the disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Health Screening -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: The screening test should have the highest possible sensitivity (few false-negative results) and specificity (few false-positive results). The ideal screening test will be 100% sensitive and 100% specific, identifying all patients with the disease and falsely diagnosing none. Unfortunately, no ideal test exists. For example, PSA screening for prostate cancer has a very high false-positive rate, as well as the very real possibility of diagnosing an indolent disease that may never cause symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: Diagnostic Testing Accuracy: Sensitivity, Specificity, Predictive Values and Likelihood Ratios -- Issues of Concern -- Application Exercise
retrieved_docs.content: Let’s calculate the sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, LR+, and LR-. We first can start with a 2X2 Table. The information above allows us to enter the values in the table below. Notice that values in blue cells were not provided, but we can get them based on the numbers above and the utilization of total cells. See Image. Diagnostic Testing Accuracy Table 2. The provider found that a total of 384 individuals actually had the disease, but how accurate was the blood test? Results: Sensitivity Sensitivity=(True Positives (A))/(True Positives (A)+False Negatives (C)) Sensitivity=(369 (A))/(369(A)+15 (C)) Sensitivity=369/384 Sensitivity=0.961 Specificity Specificity=(True Negatives (D))/(True Negatives (D)+False Positives (B)) Specificity=(558 (D))/(558(D)+58 (B)) Specificity=558/616 Specificity=0.906 Positive Predictive Value PPV =(True Positives (A))/(True Positives (A)+False Positives (B)) PPV =(369  (A))/(369 (A)+58(B)) PPV =369/427 PPV =0.864 Negative Predictive Value

retrieved_docs.title: Fecal Occult Blood Test -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Ingestion of red meat can cause false-positive results due to the peroxidase activity of heme in the meat. [48] The false-positive rate was higher when rehydration was used before the analysis. [35] Even after cooking the meat, some peroxidase activity can be detected. [44] Studies on healthy volunteers have shown that it takes about three days for the risk of a false-positive test due to meat ingestion to disappear. [49] While current recommendations are to avoid red meat for at least three days before testing, the time it takes for the risk of false-positive results to disappear after stopping red meat may vary in different clinical situations and with people with altered bowel movements. However, others have not found any significant effect of red meat on occult blood tests. [50]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Drug Testing -- Diagnostic Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Drug testing can be divided into two categories: screening and confirmation. Screening tests, be they point-of-care (POCT) or laboratory-based immunoassays, generally have lower sensitivity or specificity than definitive tests as they mainly serve to detect the presence of a drug in the system. The primary benefit of a screening test is a quick result that allows for immediate intervention. Therefore, presumptive tests should be used when it is a priority to have more immediate results. If the results of a speculative or screening test are disputed, the test should be confirmed using a definitive method. Definitive confirmatory tests are performed when the screening test is positive or when there is a need to test for drugs for which no immunoassays are available.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2076
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic agents for diabetes testing are a class of substances employed to evaluate glucose metabolism and detect diabetes, and tolbutamide is identified as a member of this diagnostic group.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2852
retrieved_docs.content: In clinical assessment, results that are above the reference range are classified as increased findings, and an increased finding is interpreted as indicating tachycardia (تسرع القلب).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2660
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium are classified as a subset of other diagnostic cardiovascular procedures, indicating that they belong to the broader group of cardiovascular diagnostic tests. Within this category, there is a further subdivision called Other diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium, which represents a more specific group of tests focused on the heart and its surrounding pericardial sac. This hierarchical organization clarifies how heart‑ and pericardium‑specific diagnostics fit into the overall framework of cardiovascular diagnostic testing, aiding clinicians in selecting and categorizing appropriate investigative procedures for cardiac and pericardial conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 1752
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium gluconate is contraindicated in patients with قصور الكلى (renal failure), meaning its use should be avoided in individuals suffering from kidney failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 4 -- Community 2880
retrieved_docs.content: Values that are above the reference range are classified as “Increased” (i.e., “Above reference range” isa “Increased”), and an “Increased” finding is interpreted as indicative of essential hypertension.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2469
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions are classified as a subcategory of اضطراب السبيل البولي (urinary tract disorder). These nonspecific genitourinary manifestations frequently co‑occur with حصاة حالبية (kidney stone), indicating that patients presenting with such symptoms should be evaluated for possible calculi within the urinary system. Recognizing that genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions belong to the broader umbrella of urinary tract disorders and are often associated with kidney stones helps clinicians prioritize diagnostic testing and management strategies aimed at identifying and treating underlying stone disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 286
retrieved_docs.content: زيادة الفجوة الأنيونية (increased anion gap) is clinically associated with قصور الكلى (renal failure), indicating that patients with renal failure often exhibit a widened anion gap in laboratory assessments. Consequently, magnesium gluconate is contraindicated in individuals with قصور الكلى, meaning that the use of magnesium gluconate should be avoided in patients suffering from renal failure to prevent potential adverse effects.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2614
retrieved_docs.content: Disease condition determination, uncontrolled is described as a general clinical state that is interpreted by clinicians (is_interpreted_by) and is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications. This indicates that an uncontrolled disease condition determination reflects a concerning overall clinical status and frequently co‑occurs with complicated diabetes mellitus, highlighting the need for careful assessment and targeted management in patients presenting with such uncontrolled conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1941
retrieved_docs.content: Above reference range is used as an interpretation when evaluating disorders of lipid metabolism, indicating that laboratory values exceeding the normal limits may reflect underlying lipid metabolic abnormalities. Within the category of disorders of lipid metabolism, فرط البروتينات الشحمية (hyperlipoproteinemia) is recognized as a specific subtype, representing conditions characterized by excessive circulating lipoproteins. Additionally, ارتفاع السكر في الدم/داء السكري البادِئُ الجديد (SMQ) (elevated blood glucose/new‑onset prediabetes) is considered a member of the broader disorders of lipid metabolism, highlighting the clinical interrelationship between dysglycemia and lipid metabolic disturbances. Understanding these connections assists clinicians in interpreting abnormal lipid panels, identifying hyperlipoproteinemic states, and recognizing the metabolic overlap with early glucose dysregulation for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2896
retrieved_docs.content: Cardiac function is a broad physiological concept that includes measures such as معدل ضربات القلب (heart rate). In clinical practice, the evaluation of cardiac function is interpreted specifically in the context of congestive heart failure in patients who are non‑hypertensive, providing important information for diagnosis and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1610
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic agents for bile duct patency testing and gastric secretion test diagnostic agents are both classified as “other diagnostic agents in ATC.” These agents are used for specialized diagnostic procedures: the former assess the openness of the bile ducts, while the latter evaluate gastric secretory function. By being grouped under the broader ATC category of other diagnostic agents, they share a common regulatory and therapeutic classification despite serving distinct clinical testing purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 1886
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions are noted to co‑occur with حصاة حالبية and also with سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي, indicating that patients who present with vague or nonspecific genitourinary complaints may simultaneously have ureteral stones (حصاة حالبية) or prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي). Recognizing this association can prompt clinicians to consider targeted diagnostic evaluations for these specific urologic pathologies when faced with ambiguous genitourinary presentations, thereby improving detection and management of underlying stone disease or malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1761
retrieved_docs.content: When laboratory results are reported as “Above reference range,” clinicians interpret this finding as indicative of a disorder of lipid metabolism. Hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية) is a specific condition that falls under the broader category of disorders of lipid metabolism. Recognizing that an above‑reference‑range result may signal hyperlipoproteinemia helps guide further diagnostic evaluation and appropriate management of patients with abnormal lipid profiles.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 2 -- Community 1036
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium are classified as a subset of other diagnostic cardiovascular procedures, indicating that they belong to the broader group of cardiovascular diagnostic tests. Within the diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium category, there are further subcategories, including Other diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium, as well as the specific procedure خزعة التأمور (pericardial biopsy). In parallel, the category Operation on heart and pericardium is described as having a broader relationship with خزعة التأمور, meaning that the pericardial biopsy is also considered a type of cardiac or pericardial operation. This hierarchical organization shows that خزعة التأمور functions both as a diagnostic test and as an operative intervention on the heart and pericardium, highlighting its dual role in clinical assessment and therapeutic management of cardiac and pericardial conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2216
retrieved_docs.content: Medium chain triglycerides are contraindicated in patients who have ارتفاع نسبة الدهون الثلاثية في الدم (elevated blood triglyceride levels), indicating that the use of medium chain triglycerides should be avoided in individuals with this condition because it may exacerbate or is not recommended for managing high triglyceride concentrations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2345
retrieved_docs.content: Hyperkalemia (فرطُ بوتاسيوم الدَّم) is clinically associated with low blood glucose (نقص تركيز السكر), and it frequently co‑occurs with acidosis (الحماض). This interrelationship indicates that patients who present with hyperkalemia should be evaluated for possible hypoglycemia and metabolic acidosis, as the simultaneous presence of these disturbances can affect diagnosis, treatment decisions, and overall patient management.
